---
title: "Chart Review"
---

::: {.patient-banner}
# Cindy Carlson

**92-year-old female** | **DOB:** 09/10/1932 | **Diagnosis:** Right MCA Ischemic Stroke
:::

::: {.warning}
**SLP Task:** Review the chart and identify information relevant to cognitive-communication assessment, safety, functional participation, and discharge planning.
:::

## Patient Snapshot

| Field | Information |
|---|---|
| Name | Cindy Carlson |
| DOB | 09/10/1932 |
| Sex | Female |
| Date of Admission | 10/12/2024 |
| Symptom Onset | 10/11/2024 |
| Primary Diagnosis | Right MCA ischemic stroke |
| Reason for SLP Consult | Cognitive-communication evaluation |
| Prior Level of Function | Independent with ADLs, cognitively intact |

## Chief Concern

::: {.note-box}
Left-sided neglect, right gaze preference, fluctuating orientation, memory concerns, and difficulty with speech characterized by phonemic paraphasias.
:::

## History of Present Illness

::: {.note-box}
Cindy Carlson presented to the emergency department with sudden onset confusion, left-sided weakness, and difficulty speaking. Symptoms began approximately two hours prior to arrival. Initial imaging revealed an acute ischemic stroke affecting the right MCA territory.

Current clinical concerns include left-sided neglect, right gaze preference, fluctuating orientation, memory deficits for recent events, and reduced functional awareness.
:::

## Imaging / Testing

| Test | Findings |
|---|---|
| CT Head | Acute infarct in the right MCA distribution |
| MRI Brain | Ischemic stroke involving right temporal and parietal lobes |
| Carotid Doppler | Moderate stenosis of right internal carotid artery |
| Echocardiogram | No evidence of embolic source |

## Past Medical History

| Condition | Status |
|---|---|
| Type 2 diabetes mellitus | Active |
| Hypertension | Active |
| Hyperlipidemia | Active |

## Medications

| Medication | Dose / Frequency | Clinical Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Aspirin | 81 mg daily | Stroke prevention |
| Atorvastatin | 40 mg daily | Hyperlipidemia / stroke prevention |
| Lisinopril | 20 mg daily | Blood pressure management |
| Metformin | 500 mg twice daily | Diabetes management |
| Clopidogrel | 75 mg daily | Antiplatelet therapy |

## Social History

| Area | Information |
|---|---|
| Occupation | Retired elementary school teacher |
| Living Situation | Lives with spouse; married 50 years |
| Substance Use | Non-smoker; no alcohol use reported |
| Interests | Gardening, church community |

## Medical Assessment / Plan

::: {.info}
**Right MCA ischemic stroke:** Continue medical management and monitor neurological status.  
**Left-sided neglect:** Occupational therapy to address visual-spatial deficits.  
**Cognitive deficits:** Cognitive therapy and monitoring.  
**Discharge planning:** Anticipate rehabilitation needs and arrange family education with spouse.
:::

## Chart Review Questions

1. What findings suggest possible cognitive-communication impairment?
2. What information is most relevant to determining whether Cindy is appropriate for MoCA administration?
3. What functional activities may be impacted by right hemisphere stroke?
4. What questions would you ask nursing, OT, PT, or the spouse before completing the assessment?

## Continue

[Go to Interdisciplinary Notes](interdisciplinary.qmd)
